mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending, which drives up GDP growth expectations and pushes longer-term yields higher as the market prices in stronger future demand and potential inflationary pressure.
failure_condition: A positive month-over-month increase in RSAFS is followed by a decrease in the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days in more than 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) tightens the labor market, raising expectations of wage growth and inflation, which leads investors to demand higher nominal yields on long-term Treasuries.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE falls below 7%, the 10-year yield does not rise over the next 20 trading days in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed funds rate is exceptionally low (near zero) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising, investors anticipate eventual policy normalization, causing a steepening of the yield curve and upward pressure on the 10-year yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.1% and CPIAUCSL increasing over previous 3 months
regime_hint: zero lower bound environment with rising inflation
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, the 10-year yield fails to rise over the subsequent 60 trading days in at least 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals stronger economic growth and higher long-term interest rates due to increased capital demand and latent inflation pressures.
failure_condition: During expansion phases (GDPC1 growing over 2% annualized), a positive INDPRO change is followed by a yield decline over 20 days in more than half of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals a pickup in residential investment, a leading indicator of economic activity that quickly boosts growth expectations and pushes 10-year yields higher within a short horizon.
failure_condition: A month-over-month increase in HOUST is followed by a decline in the 10-year yield over the next 5 trading days in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes the real return on fixed-income securities, prompting investors to demand higher nominal yields on 10-year Treasury notes to compensate for expected future inflation.
failure_condition: A positive year-over-year increase in CPIAUCSL is followed by a decline in the 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days in more than 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS above 200,000 per month) indicates a tight labor market that boosts income and spending, leading to higher growth and inflation expectations that push yields upward.
failure_condition: In months when PAYEMS increases by more than 200,000, the 10-year yield fails to rise over the next 20 trading days in at least half of the occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of real GDP (GDPC1) relative to its recent trend indicates above-trend economic output, which creates excess demand pressure that lifts long-term interest rates as the market anticipates tighter monetary policy.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is above its trailing 4-quarter moving average
failure_condition: When GDPC1 exceeds its 4-quarter moving average, the 10-year yield declines over the next 60 trading days in more than 50% of instances.
